exemplar.title: How does the corrosion rate of guitar strings affect their resonance frequencies?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does the corrosion rate of guitar strings affect their resonance frequencies?

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Topic and purpose are communicated clearly and consistently throughout the report.
The research question is explicitly stated, narrow in scope, and strongly drives the investigation.
Methodology is thorough, with appropriate apparatus, detailed procedures, and controlledâvariables tables.
weaknesses: Occasional digressions (e.g., broad engineering implications) dilute the sharp focus of the investigation.
Key assumptions (e.g., constant tension despite corrosion) are noted but not fully justified.
Terminology for the independent variable could be made more precise (e.g., explicitly naming electrolyte concentration).
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates accurate and effective communication of topic and purpose, a clearly focused research question, and a largely complete, wellâselected methodology. Minor lapsesâoccasional digressions and insufficient justification of some assumptionsâpreclude the top mark but firmly place it at band 5.

strengths: Shows solid command of wave mechanics (Mersenneâs law), Faradayâs law, and corrosion chemistry in interpreting data.
Calculations of corrosion rate, uncertainty propagation, and graph linearisation are mostly correct and well applied.
Terminology (resonance, antinode, Tafel plot, corrosion current density) is generally used appropriately.
weaknesses: Unit inconsistencies (e.g., mixing cm/year, mg, hours) introduce potential confusion.
Occasional slips in terminology (e.g., âcorrosivityâ vs. âcorrosion rateâ) and placeholder variables in formulas.
Constants (e.g., k in Mersenneâs law) are introduced without clear definition of physical meaning or units.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates good subject knowledge with mostly accurate application of theory and concepts, placing the work comfortably in the 3â4 band. However, inconsistent units and a few terminology errors prevent elevating it into the highest band.

strengths: Data are analysed rigorously: log and linearised plots include RÂ² values, gradients and propagated uncertainties.
The argument flows logically from theoretical background through method to data interpretation and comparison with Mersenneâs law.
Critical evaluation quantifies uncertainty and identifies methodological limitations (e.g., human hearing precision, multimeter error).
weaknesses: Some reasoning leapsâsuch as equating ln R to 1/âÎ¼âare asserted without full derivation.
Alternative explanations for outliers and broader sources of error are noted but not deeply explored.
Proposed improvements lack detailed justification and prioritisation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation shows very strong analytical work and coherent argument development, with substantial critical evaluation. While a few aspects stop short of fully sophisticated critique, overall performance sits at the top end of the 7â9 band and justifies a score of 10 out of 12.

strengths: Report follows conventions with title page, contents, headings, tables, graphs, and bibliography.
Figures and tables are generally well labelled and support the readerâs understanding.
Overall layout and formatting effectively guide navigation and comprehension.
weaknesses: Inconsistent page numbering and missing page references in the table of contents.
Residual prompt text appears in the implications section and should be removed.
A few visuals (e.g., controlledâvariables table) spill across pages or lack header repetition.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Despite minor inconsistencies, the structure and visual presentation meet required conventions and support clear communication. The cumulative effect of these elements justifies the maximum presentation score of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: Physics and electrochemistry terms â resonance, antinode, Tafel plot, corrosion current density, etc. â are mostly used correctly and consistently.  Occasional slips (âcorrosivityâ for âcorrosion rateâ, duplicated variable labels) and some stylistic inconsistencies prevent the uniform accuracy expected for the 5-6 band, but the terminology remains largely appropriate and demonstrates solid understanding.

The essay develops a logical argument: theory â method â data â derived relationship â comparison with Mersenneâs law â conclusion.  Paragraphing and headings support coherence.  However, some reasoning leaps (e.g., equating ln R response to 1/âÎ¼ without derivation) and limited engagement with contradictory data (outliers) reduce sophistication; thus a solid, but not exemplary, performance within the 4-6 band.

The student outlines detailed procedures, variables tables, apparatus lists, safety measures, and uncertainty treatment.  Use of electrolysis to control corrosion and a sonometer to measure frequency is appropriate and shows informed selection.  Some methodological weaknesses (subjective auditory matching, limited trials, identical string assumption) are acknowledged but not fully resolved, preventing a perfect score; nevertheless the methodology is largely complete and suitable.

The essay demonstrates good command of wave mechanics, Mersenneâs law, Faradayâs law, and corrosion chemistry, applying them to interpret data.  Calculations of corrosion rate, uncertainty propagation, and graph linearisation are generally correct.  However, unit handling (e.g., cm per year vs hundred cm per year), and an over-simplified link between ln R and 1/âÎ¼ indicate partial rather than complete mastery; thus the work fits comfortably in the 3-4 band, near its upper end.

The student identifies methodological flaws (human hearing, multimeter issues) and quantifies the 19 % uncertainty in k, commenting on reliability.  Evaluation is present but remains brief and largely generic; proposed improvements are not deeply justified, and theoretical critique of assumptions is limited.  This aligns with a "good" but not "excellent" level, so the lower end of the 4-6 band is appropriate.

The student states the topic and its purpose in the opening page and revisits it in the introduction, background, and conclusion.  Key phrases â âcorrosion rate of guitar stringsâ and âresonance frequenciesâ â are used consistently, and the reader can easily follow what is being investigated.  Minor lapses (e.g., occasional digressions about broader engineering implications) slightly dilute the focus, keeping it from the very top of the band, but overall communication is clear, accurate, and purposeful.

Figures, tables, and equation formatting generally support comprehension, and most visuals are labelled.  Resolution and scaling of some images are low, and a few duplicate headings distort layout, yet the presentation remains effective overall.  These minor imperfections place the work in the 3-4 band with a score of 3.

The research question is explicitly stated as âHow does the corrosion rate of guitar strings affect their resonance frequencies?â  It is sufficiently narrow (one material, one measurable acoustic output) and drives the structure of the investigation, analysis, and evaluation.  Although the hypothesis wavers between inverse and direct relationships, the question itself remains focused and clearly guides discussion; hence a strong, though not flawless, performance in the top band.

Data are processed into tables, logarithmic and linearised graphs with RÂ², gradients, and propagated uncertainties.  The analysis is clearly tied to the research question and leads to a quantitative model.  Yet depth is moderate (only seven data points, limited exploration of alternative explanations), so the strand sits mid-band (4-6) rather than the sophisticated 7-9 range.

The essay follows EE conventions: title page, abstract substitute, contents, headings, tables, graphs, bibliography.  Pagination, some table borders, and word-count placement are inconsistent, and section numbering is occasionally erratic, but overall structure remains clear and helps the reader navigate; hence it fits the 3-4 band, nearer its lower edge.

annotations.comment: The research question is crisply stated, clearly guiding the investigation and focusing on the relationship between corrosion rate and resonance frequency.
A word-count heading is present but lacks the actual numerical count. Include the precise word total to meet IB requirements.
No page number appears in the footer. Number all pages to ensure navigability and compliance with IB formatting rules.
The detailed table of contents demonstrates strong structural planning and aids reader orientation.
The TOC omits page references. Add page numbers next to each entry to improve navigation.
The introduction effectively conveys personal engagement by linking the studentâs violin experience to the study of standing waves.
The third paragraph broadens to civil engineering applications, diluting focus on guitar strings. Restrict such context to evaluation or future work.
The background section uses a clear labeled figure to illustrate standing waves, supporting conceptual understanding.
Citation style is inconsistent (e.g., some quotes lack page numbers or uniform formatting). Standardize referencing format throughout.
Constants k in the Mersenneâs laws are introduced without defining their physical meaning or units. Add an explanation of k.
Equations lack unit annotations on constants and variables. Include units inline for clarity and dimensional consistency.
The corrosion-rate formula uses placeholder âDATAâ instead of the proper variables (D, A, T). Correct to \(R_M = \frac{W}{D A T}\).
The derivation of Faradayâs law and its adaptation to corrosion rate is thorough, demonstrating solid understanding of electrochemical principles.
Density is given in gÂ·cmâ»Â³ while mass loss is in mg and time in hours. Convert all to consistent SI units to avoid confusion.
The ChatGPT prompt text remains visible in the implications section. Remove this and rewrite a concise real-world implications paragraph.
The real-world implications are well-motivated but would be stronger if supported by quantitative examples or literature.
Assumption that tension remains unaffected by corrosion needs justificationâdiscuss how material loss might alter tension.
Controlled-variables table is comprehensive, demonstrating careful experimental control over tension, time, length, and voltage.
The controlled-variables table spills across pages without repeating headers. Consider fitting it on one page or repeating column titles.
The symbol \(\overline{a}\) for the independent variable is introduced without definition. Remove or clearly define all notation.
Apparatus list is detailed and demonstrates informed selection of equipment relevant to both corrosion and acoustic measurements.
In the apparatus list, the balanceâs precision is shown as ââ 0.1 gâ. Correct the symbol to âÂ±â for clarity.
Clarify the assumption that human hearing produces sufficiently precise frequency matches, perhaps by quantifying expected error.
The independent variable description would benefit from explicitly naming it âelectrolyte concentrationâ to align with later sections.
